ref: Abraham and Sarah were very old, and Sarah was well past the age for having children.
text: Ibrahim ni Saara zaa daa n kurigini, haali, Saara daa n bi lee n pegira.
translated: Ibrahim and Sara are together now, but Sara is not like him.

ref: Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah to meet him in the pasture where he kept his sheep,
text: Yakubu ni wumi n ŋa, o tolimi n yeli Rachel ni Liya, ni ba ta o saani, seela o ni buŋkobiri n be la.
translated: Yakubu is my father; he married Rachel and Leah, and they have children, living together in the house.

ref: Jacob sent messengers on ahead to Esau, who lived in the land of Seir, also known as Edom.
text: Niŋi n na, Yakubu tumi o tumusi, ba pa yii we o beeri Isawu saani, a yi Seya n ba Idom paaligu.
translated: If I am, Yakubu will go, and we will meet at Isawu, and we will see Seya in Idom.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Ka Naawunni lee n yeli, “Cheli ti maali nisaaladima, ba ŋmaani ti, ka mari daama, a yi koom puu koroto niŋi, niinsi, a ni tiŋgbaŋŋu buŋkobiri zaa niŋi.”
translated: When they welcomed me, they said, “We are glad you have come, come inside, there is food, sit down, and we will talk about the story of the ancestors.”

translated: I’m sorry, but I don’t have enough information about the Hanga language to provide an accurate translation. If you can share more context or a source for the language, I’ll do my best to help.

ref: I want you and your descendants to have many children, so people will live everywhere on earth.
text: “Ama nyiwune, dogi bidamta, lala jemani ya yaŋsi ni paali tiŋgbaŋaa zaa niŋi.”
translated: Mother is cooking, father is working, the child is playing with a ball in the garden at night.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Niŋi n na, Tera mari o bii Abram ni o yaŋŋu Loti, Haran biya, o deempoga Sarai, Abram poga, a nyisigi n yi Waa n ba Babilon paaligu, a wara Keenan paaligu. Ba n wara maa, ba wa ti paa seela ba n boona Haran, ka zi niŋi.
translated: Now, Abram’s father and his mother Loti were in Haran; his wife Sarai was there, Abram went, and they traveled to the land of Babylon and also to the land of Canaan. They went there, stayed for a time in Haran, and that is what happened.
